questions.text: Which viruses enter the host cell as a whole?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Plant viruses cannot enter as whole particles due to the tough cell wall. Instead, they rely on mechanical injury or insect vectors to deliver only their genome (e.g., tobacco mosaic virus).
Bacteriophages do not enter entirely. They attach to bacteria and inject their genetic material, while the protein capsid stays outside (e.g., T4 phage).

Once attached to a host cell, animal viruses may enter in a variety of ways: by endocytosis, where the membrane folds in; by making channels in the host membrane (through which DNA or RNA can be injected); or, for enveloped viruses, by fusing with the membrane and releasing the capsid inside the cell.
This option is incorrect. An animal virus can enter the host cell as a whole particle.

questions.explanation: Animal viruses can enter host cells as whole particles because animal cells lack rigid walls, using processes like membrane fusion or endocytosis before uncoating their genome. In contrast, plant viruses cannot enter as complete virions due to the tough cell wall; they depend on wounds or insect vectors to introduce only their genome. Bacteriophages also do not enter entirely—they attach to bacteria and inject their genetic material while the capsid remains outside.

questions.text: Cytochrome c reduces which coenzyme in the ETC?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cytochrome c transfers electrons to cytochrome a, which is a component of Complex IV (cytochrome c oxidase) in the electron transport chain. This is the correct downstream acceptor in the chain after cytochrome c.

There is no such component as "cytochrome q" in the ETC. This option may be confused with Coenzyme Q (ubiquinone), which comes before cytochrome c in the ETC, not after. Also, coenzyme Q accepts electrons from Complex I and II, not from cytochrome c.

Cytochrome b is a part of Complex III (cytochrome bc1 complex) and is upstream of cytochrome c. Cytochrome c actually receives electrons from cytochrome b (via cytochrome c1), not the other way around. So, cytochrome c does not reduce cytochrome b.
While cytochrome a3 is part of Complex IV and receives electrons after cytochrome a, cytochrome c first reduces cytochrome a, not a3. So, a3 is not the direct electron acceptor from cytochrome c.

questions.explanation: In the ETC, electrons are passed down a chain of carriers from higher to lower energy states. Cytochrome c is a mobile electron carrier that shuttles electrons from Complex III to Complex IV. Within Complex IV, cytochrome a is the first molecule that accepts electrons from cytochrome c. Therefore, cytochrome a is the correct answer, while the other options are either upstream components, not directly involved, or do not exist in this context.

questions.text: In peas, the gene for yellow color (C) is dominant to the gene for green color (c). To determine the genotype of an unknown yellow pea, what kind of pea should you cross with it?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: To determine the genotype of the unknown yellow pea, you should cross it with a recessive green pea (cc).
Crossing the unknown yellow pea with any genotype won't give you information about the genotype of the yellow pea. To determine the genotype, you need to perform a specific cross.

If you cross the unknown yellow pea with a homozygous dominant (CC) yellow pea, all the offspring will have the yellow color (C_) phenotype. However, this cross won't allow you to determine the genotype of the unknown yellow pea, as it could be either CC or Cc.
To determine the genotype of the unknown yellow pea, you should cross it with a recessive green pea (cc).

questions.explanation: A green pea would definitely be homozygous recessive. A is the best option because if the unknown dominant individual is heterozygous, then the offspring of this year's cross will include some recessive individuals. If the unknown is homozygous, then all offspring will be dominant heterozygous. In this way, crossing an unknown dominant phenotype with a recessive individual is the surest way of determining the unknown genotype.

questions.text: In the plants, there are 50 or more thylakoids piled upon each other to form?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In plants, thylakoids are flattened, disc-shaped structures found in the chloroplasts, and they contain the photosynthetic pigments responsible for capturing light energy. A stack of thylakoids is known as a granum (plural: grana). Grana are organized in the stroma of chloroplasts and play a crucial role in the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis.
The centrosome is a structure found in animal cells, not in plants. It is involved in cell division and is responsible for organizing the microtubules during mitosis and meiosis.

The stroma is the gel-like fluid that surrounds the thylakoids in chloroplasts. It contains enzymes and other molecules necessary for the light-independent reactions of photosynthesis (also known as the Calvin cycle).

The term 'multinucleate' refers to a cell that contains multiple nuclei. It is not related to the organization of thylakoids in plants. Plant cells typically have a single large central vacuole and a single nucleus.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Granum. In plants, thylakoids are organized into stacks called grana, which are essential for the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis. These reactions occur in the thylakoid membranes, where light energy is converted into chemical energy. The other options are incorrect: a Centrosome is found in animal cells, not plant cells; the Stroma is the fluid surrounding the thylakoids in chloroplasts, not a stack of them; and Multinucleate refers to cells with multiple nuclei, which is unrelated to thylakoid organization.

questions.text: The neurons that interpret and receive information and stimulate motor neurons are what type of neurons?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sensory neurons, also known as afferent neurons, are responsible for carrying sensory information from sensory organs (such as the eyes, ears, skin, etc.) to the central nervous system (brain and spinal cord). These neurons detect various stimuli from the environment, such as light, sound, touch, and temperature.

Motor neurons, also known as efferent neurons, are responsible for carrying signals from the central nervous system to the effectors (muscles and glands) to produce a response. They play a crucial role in controlling muscle movements and glandular secretions.

Interneurons, also called association neurons, are found within the central nervous system (CNS). They act as a bridge between sensory neurons and motor neurons. Interneurons receive information from sensory neurons, process and interpret that information, and then transmit signals to motor neurons, which eventually produce a response.
There is no specific type of neuron called "rotator neurons." It might be a term used in some contexts, but it is not a standard classification of neurons.

questions.explanation: The body has sensory neurons, which deliver information to the CNS, motor neurons that distribute commands to peripheral effectors, and interneurons, which interpret information and coordinate responses. Hence, the answer is C.

questions.text: Lecithin contains _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ethanolamine is a compound belonging to the class of organic compounds known as ethanolamines. It is an amine compound that has two hydroxyl groups and one amino group attached to a three-carbon chain. Ethanolamine is not typically found in lecithin.

The correct answer is b) Choline. Lecithin is a phospholipid that contains choline as its head group. Choline is an essential nutrient and is crucial for the formation and structure of cell membranes.
Serine is an amino acid and is not a component of lecithin. However, serine is a part of other phospholipids found in cell membranes.
Betaine is a derivative of the amino acid glycine and is not a component of lecithin.

questions.explanation: Lecithin is a phospholipid which consists of glycerol, two fatty acids, a phosphate group and choline. Choline is the only one in the options so the answer is B.

questions.text: A long polypeptide that is cut up to generate several small proteins is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The correct answer is a) Polyprotein. A long polypeptide that is cut up to generate several small proteins is known as a polyprotein. Polyproteins are large precursor proteins that are translated from a single mRNA and later cleaved into individual functional proteins by proteolytic enzymes.

Polymerases are enzymes responsible for catalyzing the synthesis of polymers, such as DNA or RNA, from their monomers (nucleotides). They are not involved in the process of cutting up long polypeptides.

Polyisomerases are not directly related to the process of cutting up long polypeptides. Isomerases are enzymes that catalyze the interconversion of isomers, but polyisomerase is not a commonly known term in biology.

options.text: None of these options is correct
options.explanation: This option is not correct because the correct answer is a) Polyprotein, as explained above.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is a) Polyprotein. A long polypeptide that is cut up to generate several small proteins is known as a polyprotein. Polyproteins are large precursor proteins that are translated from a single mRNA and later cleaved into individual functional proteins by proteolytic enzymes.

questions.text: The central nervous system consists of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The brain is a part of the central nervous system (CNS). It is a highly complex organ that serves as the control center of the body. It receives and processes sensory information, regulates voluntary and involuntary actions, and is responsible for memory, learning, and emotions.

The spinal cord is another vital component of the central nervous system. It is a long, cylindrical bundle of nerves that extends from the base of the brain down the vertebral canal. The spinal cord acts as a communication pathway between the brain and the rest of the body. It relays sensory information to the brain and sends motor commands from the brain to the body's muscles and organs.

Nerves and ganglia are parts of the peripheral nervous system (PNS), not the central nervous system. The PNS consists of nerves that connect the central nervous system to various body parts, including sensory receptors, muscles, and glands. Ganglia are clusters of nerve cell bodies located outside the central nervous system.

options.text: Both Options A and B are correct
options.explanation: This option is correct. The central nervous system consists of both the brain and the spinal cord. Together, they form the primary control center for the body's functions.

questions.explanation: The central nervous system (CNS) is the processing center for the nervous system. The two main organs of the CNS are the brain and spinal cord. The brain processes and interprets the sensory information sent from the spinal cord. Both the brain and spinal cord are protected by three layers of connective tissue, called meninges. Within the central nervous system is a system of hollow cavities, called 'ventricles'. The network of linked cavities in the brain (cerebral ventricles) is continuous with the central canal of the spinal cord. The ventricles are filled with cerebrospinal fluid, which is produced by specialized epithelium located within the ventricles, called the choroid plexus. Cerebrospinal fluid surrounds, cushions, and protects the brain and spinal cord from trauma. It also assists in the circulation of nutrients to the brain.

questions.text: From one pyruvate passing through the Krebs cycle, how many NADH are formed?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. The Krebs cycle for one pyruvate produces more than one NADH. This option underestimates the actual yield.
Incorrect. Although some steps in the cycle yield NADH, two molecules is not the total output for one pyruvate.

The Krebs cycle is the second phase of aerobic respiration. Since glycolysis produces two pyruvates and hence two acetyl-CoAs from one molecule of glucose, the net yield of NADH₂ from the Krebs cycle for one glucose is 6. For one pyruvate, one acetyl-CoA condenses to form citrate, which undergoes oxidative decarboxylation, forming 3 molecules of NADH₂ and one molecule of FADH₂.
Incorrect. The total production of NADH for one pyruvate does not reach four; it is actually three.

questions.explanation: From one molecule of pyruvate passing through the Krebs cycle, three molecules of NADH are formed. During the cycle, pyruvate is first converted into acetyl-CoA, which then enters the Krebs cycle. As the acetyl-CoA is oxidized, hydrogen atoms are transferred to NAD⁺, forming NADH at three different steps. These NADH molecules later carry high-energy electrons to the electron transport chain, where they are used to produce ATP. Thus, each pyruvate generates three NADH molecules in the Krebs cycle.

questions.text: Viruses can bud from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viruses can bud from the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) when they are replicating in host cells. Some viruses, like flaviviruses, use the RER for the assembly and maturation of their viral particles before they are released from the infected cell.

Viruses can also bud from the Golgi complex, also known as the Golgi apparatus. The Golgi complex plays a role in modifying and packaging various molecules within the cell, and some viruses hijack this cellular machinery to assemble their viral particles and exit the cell.

Some viruses, particularly those with large DNA genomes, can bud from the nuclear envelope of the host cell. These viruses replicate their genetic material in the cell nucleus and then utilize the nuclear envelope for budding and exiting the nucleus.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: This option is correct. Different viruses have different strategies for budding and exiting host cells, and they can use various cellular structures like the rough endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi complex, and nuclear envelope for their assembly and release.

questions.explanation: Viruses can bud at every stage in the ER-Golgi-cell membrane pathway. As the nucleocapsids assembled or in the process of being built, induce the formation of a membrane curvature in the host cell membrane and wrap up in the forming bud which is eventually pinched off by membrane scission to release the enveloped particle.

questions.text: Chitinous Setae are the locomotory organs of annelids which are present on?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cell wall is a rigid structure that provides support and protection to plant cells, bacteria, and some fungi. In annelids (segmented worms), chitinous setae are not present on the cell wall. Setae are specialized bristle-like structures found on the external surface of the body of annelids that aid in locomotion and provide traction.

The prostomium is the first segment of the body in some annelids, located at the anterior end (head region). It often bears sensory structures like eyes and tentacles. However, chitinous setae are not found on the prostomium.
The nucleolus is an organelle found inside the cell nucleus and is involved in the production of ribosomes. It is not related to the presence of chitinous setae in annelids.

The correct answer is d) Parapodia. Parapodia are fleshy, paddle-like structures that extend from the sides of the body segments in many annelids. They are specialized for locomotion, respiration, and sometimes serve as gills. Chitinous setae are present on the parapodia, and they play a crucial role in helping the annelids move through their environment by providing grip and traction.

They are specialized for locomotion, respiration, and sometimes serve as gills. questions.text: Which of the following is true about skeletal muscle fibres?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Better adapted for slow, sustained activities
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Smooth muscles are more suited for slow, sustained activities due to their ability to maintain contractions over long periods without fatigue.

options.text: For energy, they depend on fatty acids
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Skeletal muscles primarily utilize glucose and glycogen for energy, especially during intense activities, rather than relying predominantly on fatty acids.
This statement is correct. Skeletal muscles have a higher myoglobin content compared to other muscle types, which facilitates oxygen storage and enhances endurance during physical activity.
This is incorrect. Skeletal muscles contain a significant number of mitochondria, which are essential for aerobic respiration and energy production, especially in oxidative fibers.

questions.explanation: Option A is incorrect as smooth muscles are specifically adapted for prolonged, low-energy tasks, while skeletal muscles are designed for quick, powerful movements. Option B is false because skeletal muscles primarily rely on glucose and glycogen for energy during activity rather than fatty acids. Option C is correct; skeletal muscles contain high levels of myoglobin, which aids in oxygen storage and delivery during contraction, enhancing their performance. Option D is incorrect, as skeletal muscles have a substantial number of mitochondria, especially in oxidative muscle fibers, which are crucial for energy production during sustained activity.

questions.text: When a hemophilia carrier woman marries a normal man, who among her offspring may be affected:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: If a hemophilia carrier woman (heterozygous for the hemophilia gene) marries a normal man (homozygous for the normal gene), there is a chance that all her children may inherit the hemophilia gene. However, the probability of each child being affected or a carrier depends on the genotype of the woman.

If the woman is a carrier (heterozygous), there is a 50% chance that each of her daughters will inherit the hemophilia gene. Daughters inherit one X chromosome from their mother, and it is a random process which X chromosome (either with the hemophilia gene or the normal gene) is passed on.
This option is similar to option b) and is incorrect. It is unlikely that all her daughters will be affected by hemophilia, as the inheritance pattern is random.

If the woman is a carrier (heterozygous), there is a 50% chance that each of her sons will inherit the hemophilia gene. Sons inherit one X chromosome from their mother, and they receive the Y chromosome from their father. Since the father is normal (XY), sons have a 50% chance of inheriting the hemophilia gene from their mother (XH) and a 50% chance of inheriting the normal gene (X).

questions.explanation: Here it shows that 25% of her children will be affected and 50% of her sons so the answer is D.

questions.text: Porcupine is a mammal because:

questions.text: Alkanes are called as _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Olefines, also known as olefins or alkenes, are a class of hydrocarbons that contain at least one carbon-carbon double bond. Unlike alkanes, they are unsaturated hydrocarbons because they have fewer hydrogen atoms per carbon atom due to the presence of double bonds.

Paraffins are another name for alkanes. They are a class of saturated hydrocarbons consisting of only single carbon-carbon bonds and carbon-hydrogen bonds. The term "paraffin" is commonly used in the context of petroleum products, where it refers to a mixture of alkanes.

This option is not applicable to alkanes. Alkanes are generally considered unreactive, especially in comparison to alkenes (olefins) and alkynes, which are more reactive due to the presence of double and triple bonds, respectively.

Paraffin comes from the Latin term 'param affinum' meaning 'less affinity' in other words ‘less reactivity’. Alkanes have only single bonds which are sigma covalent, thus alkanes are called paraffin because they have a lesser affinity toward general reagents.

questions.text: Thermal energy is also called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Internal energy is the sum of all the kinetic and potential energies of the particles within a system. It includes both the kinetic energy of the particles due to their motion and the potential energy due to their positions relative to each other. Thermal energy is a part of internal energy that specifically refers to the kinetic energy associated with the random motion of particles.

Temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of the particles in a substance. It gives an indication of how hot or cold a body is relative to a reference point (such as the Celsius or Kelvin scale). However, it is not the same as thermal energy, as thermal energy depends on both temperature and the amount of substance present.

Kinetic energy is the energy possessed by an object or system due to its motion. While thermal energy is related to the kinetic energy of the particles, it specifically refers to the total kinetic energy of all particles in a substance.

Heat energy is the energy transferred between two substances at different temperatures due to their temperature difference. It is the energy exchanged during heating or cooling processes. Heat energy is also called thermal energy.

questions.explanation: Thermal energy is also called heat energy. Heat energy is another term for thermal energy, kinetic energy is a property of a moving object, and internal energy is the total amount of kinetic energy and potential energy in a system.

questions.text: Which of the following properties does not belong to Gases?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Gases do not have a definite volume. Unlike liquids and solids, the particles in a gas are widely spaced and move freely, occupying the entire container they are in. The volume of a gas can change with changes in temperature and pressure.

Gases also do not have a definite shape. The particles in a gas move randomly and spread out to fill the entire available space of the container. They do not have a fixed arrangement like the particles in solids, which have a definite shape.

Gases generally have low density compared to liquids and solids. This is because the particles in gases are spread out and have more empty space between them. As a result, gases are much less dense than liquids and solids.

This option does not belong to gases. In gases, the particles have weak interactions with each other. The attractive forces between gas particles are typically much weaker compared to those in liquids and solids. This is why gases can easily compress and expand, and their particles can move freely.

questions.explanation: Strong interactions as gases have weak interactions since the particles move quickly in random motion and move far apart from each other which results in weak intermolecular forces of attraction.

questions.text: Each half-reaction in the ion-electron method is balanced by adding:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Electrons on the left hand side
options.explanation: Electrons on the left-hand side: This is only true for reduction half-reactions.

options.text: Electrons on the ride hand side
options.explanation: Electrons on the right-hand side: This is only true for oxidation half-reactions.

options.text: Either left or right hand side
options.explanation: In the ion-electron method (also known as the half-reaction method), each half-reaction is balanced by adding electrons (e^-) to the reactants or products to balance the charges.In a redox reaction, which involves the transfer of electrons between reactants, the reaction is split into two half-reactions: the oxidation half-reaction and the reduction half-reaction. The oxidation half-reaction involves the loss of electrons, while the reduction half-reaction involves the gain of electrons.To balance these half-reactions, you need to ensure that the number of electrons lost in the oxidation half-reaction is equal to the number of electrons gained in the reduction half-reaction.For example, consider the following redox reaction:Cu(s) + 2Ag^+(aq) → Cu^2+(aq) + 2Ag(s)In this reaction, copper (Cu) is oxidized to copper ions (Cu^2+), losing two electrons. Meanwhile, silver ions (Ag^+) are reduced to solid silver (Ag), gaining two electrons.The two half-reactions are:Oxidation half-reaction: Cu(s) → Cu^2+(aq) + 2e^-Reduction half-reaction: 2Ag^+(aq) + 2e^- → 2Ag(s)To balance these half-reactions, electrons are added to the reactants or products to balance the charges. In this case, two electrons are added to the oxidation half-reaction, and two electrons are added to the reduction half-reaction:Balanced oxidation half-reaction: Cu(s) → Cu^2+(aq) + 2e^-Balanced reduction half-reaction: 2Ag^+(aq) + 2e^- → 2Ag(s)Now, the number of electrons lost in the oxidation half-reaction (2e^-) is equal to the number of electrons gained in the reduction half-reaction (2e^-), ensuring the overall conservation of charge in the redox reaction.

options.text: None of these options is correct
options.explanation: This is not the correct answer, as option (c) is correct.

questions.explanation: Explanation has been added. In the ion-electron method (also known as the half-reaction method), each half-reaction is balanced by adding electrons (e^-) to the reactants or products to balance the charges. In a redox reaction, which involves the transfer of electrons between reactants, the reaction is split into two half-reactions: the oxidation half-reaction and the reduction half-reaction. The oxidation half-reaction involves the loss of electrons, while the reduction half-reaction involves the gain of electrons. To balance these half-reactions, you need to ensure that the number of electrons lost in the oxidation half-reaction is equal to the number of electrons gained in the reduction half-reaction. For example, consider the following redox reaction: Cu(s) + 2Ag^+(aq) → Cu^2+(aq) + 2Ag(s) In this reaction, copper (Cu) is oxidized to copper ions (Cu^2+), losing two electrons. Meanwhile, silver ions (Ag^+) are reduced to solid silver (Ag), gaining two electrons. The two half-reactions are: Oxidation half-reaction: Cu(s) → Cu^2+(aq) + 2e^- Reduction half-reaction: 2Ag^+(aq) + 2e^- → 2Ag(s) To balance these half-reactions, electrons are added to the reactants or products to balance the charges. In this case, two electrons are added to the oxidation half-reaction, and two electrons are added to the reduction half-reaction: Balanced oxidation half-reaction: Cu(s) → Cu^2+(aq) + 2e^- Balanced reduction half-reaction: 2Ag^+(aq) + 2e^- → 2Ag(s) Now, the number of electrons lost in the oxidation half-reaction (2e^-) is equal to the number of electrons gained in the reduction half-reaction (2e^-), ensuring the overall conservation of charge in the redox reaction.

questions.text: The formula of Chile Saltpeter is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium nitrate is a compound with the chemical formula NaNO3. It is a type of nitrate salt and is commonly known as Chile saltpeter or soda niter. It is used in various applications, including as a food preservative, a fertilizer, and in the production of gunpowder.

Calcium carbonate is a chemical compound with the formula CaCO3. It is a common mineral found in rocks and the main component of shells of marine organisms like snails and coral reefs. It is also used as a dietary supplement, antacid, and in the production of cement and lime.

Barium nitrate is a chemical compound with the formula Ba(NO3)2. It is a colorless, odorless salt that is highly toxic. Barium nitrate is used in the production of green fireworks and signal flares due to its ability to produce bright green flames when ignited.

Ammonium chloride is a compound with the formula NH4Cl. It is a white crystalline salt commonly known as sal ammoniac. Ammonium chloride is used in various applications, including as a fertilizer, a flux in soldering, and as an expectorant in cough medicines.

questions.explanation: The formula for Chile Saltpeter is NaNO3. It is also known as Sodium nitrate. CaCO3 is calcium carbonate, Ba(NO3)2 is barium nitrate, and NH4Cl is ammonium chloride.

questions.text: To conduct electricity through Ionic Solids, the ions should be:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: In the context of ionic solids, "excited" usually refers to the state of an electron in an atom or ion that has absorbed energy and moved to a higher energy level. While the concept of electronic excitation is essential in various chemical and physical processes, it is not directly related to the conduction of electricity through ionic solids.

"Energized" generally means that a system or particle has gained energy. In the case of ionic solids, energy is indeed required to initiate the conduction of electricity, but it is not the ions themselves that need to be energized. Instead, the energy typically comes from an external source like an electric field, which allows the ions to move and conduct electricity.

The correct answer is "c) Free." For a material to conduct electricity, it must have mobile charge carriers. In ionic solids, these mobile charge carriers are free ions, which can move under the influence of an electric field. When an ionic solid is dissolved in water or melted, the ions become free and can move, resulting in electrical conductivity.

While the ions in an ionic solid do vibrate and have thermal motion, this random motion is not directly related to the conduction of electricity through the solid. Electrical conductivity in ionic solids primarily depends on the availability of free ions that can move in response to an electric field.

questions.explanation: To conduct energy through Ionic Solids, the ions should be free to move. Ionic compounds conduct electricity as liquids or when in solution since the ions are free to move. Ionic compounds do not conduct electricity in a solid state since the ions are held in place and can not move.

questions.text: In Haber's process, the final product ammonia is converted into:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ammonia is not converted into a solid state in the Haber's process. Under standard conditions, ammonia is a gas, and it is typically liquefied for separation and storage. It does not solidify under the conditions used in this process.

Ammonia is produced as a gas in the Haber's process but is typically converted into a liquid state by cooling and pressurizing to separate it from unreacted gases. This is the correct transformation mentioned in the question.

Ammonia is indeed produced in the gaseous state in the Haber's process. However, the question specifically asks about what the ammonia is converted into, which is a liquid state for easier handling and separation.

options.text: None of these options is correct
options.explanation: Option B is correct because ammonia, initially produced as a gas, is converted into a liquid state for practical purposes. This option is incorrect as it suggests none of the given transformations is correct.

questions.explanation: In the Haber's process, ammonia (NH3) is synthesized in the gaseous state from nitrogen (N2) and hydrogen (H2) under high pressure and temperature with an iron catalyst. The question focuses on what happens to ammonia after its formation. Ammonia gas is typically converted into a liquid state by cooling and pressurizing, which facilitates its separation from unreacted nitrogen and hydrogen gases and allows for easier transportation and storage. Therefore, Option B, 'Liquid state,' is correct. Option A is incorrect because ammonia is not converted into a solid state under these conditions. Option C is incorrect because, while ammonia is produced as a gas, the question asks about its conversion. Option D is incorrect because Option B provides the correct answer.

questions.text: When an electron is added, energy is released, so electron affinity is given a:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: If electron affinity is given a positive sign, it would imply that energy is absorbed when an electron is added to the atom. This would mean that the atom is less likely to gain an electron and would have low electron affinity. However, this is not the case, as energy is released when an electron is added.

Correct! When an electron is added to an atom, energy is released, signifying that the atom has a high affinity for electrons. Electron affinity is, therefore, given a negative sign to indicate the energy change associated with the addition of an electron.

"Neutral" does not directly apply to the sign of electron affinity. Electron affinity can be either positive or negative, depending on whether energy is absorbed or released, respectively, during the process.

questions.text: The compounds which belong to the same functional group form a _.

Correct! Compounds that belong to the same functional group and have a similar chemical structure, with the same general formula, are known as a homologous series. They exhibit similar chemical properties and show a gradual change in physical properties as the number of carbon atoms increases.

questions.text: Globulin proteins are found in:

questions.text: How can enthalpy changes of reactions be measured?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Spectrometry is a technique used to measure the interaction of matter with electromagnetic radiation, such as light. While it is a powerful tool for analyzing the composition of substances, it is not commonly used to directly measure enthalpy changes of reactions.

Conductometry is a method that measures the electrical conductivity of a solution or substance. It is more suited for determining the concentration of ions in a solution rather than directly measuring enthalpy changes of reactions.

Correct! Calorimetry is the most common method used to directly measure the enthalpy changes of reactions. It involves the use of a calorimeter, which is an instrument that measures the heat exchanged between a reaction and its surroundings. By carefully monitoring the temperature changes, the heat of the reaction can be calculated, which is directly related to the enthalpy change.

Refractometry is a technique used to measure the refractive index of a substance, which describes how light bends as it passes through the substance. While refractometry is useful for characterizing the optical properties of materials, it is not commonly used for measuring enthalpy changes of reactions.

questions.explanation: Calorimetry is used to measure amounts of heat change.

questions.text: Which of the following forces has ability to form clusters and large molecules?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionic bonds are formed between ions, which are charged particles resulting from the transfer of electrons from one atom to another. These bonds are relatively strong and are responsible for holding together ionic compounds, such as salts. While ionic compounds can form large structures, they do not typically form clusters or large molecules in the same way as the other forces listed.

Induced dipole forces, also known as London dispersion forces, are weak attractive forces that arise from temporary fluctuations in electron distribution within molecules. Even though they are weak individually, they can become significant when large numbers of molecules are involved. While they can lead to the formation of temporary clusters, they do not create stable large molecules.

Hydrogen bonds are special types of dipole-dipole interactions that occur when a hydrogen atom is covalently bonded to a highly electronegative atom (e.g., nitrogen, oxygen, or fluorine) and forms a weak electrostatic interaction with another electronegative atom. Hydrogen bonds are relatively strong compared to other intermolecular forces, and they can lead to the formation of stable clusters and large molecules. For example, hydrogen bonding is essential for the structure and function of DNA and proteins.

options.text: All of these options are correct.

questions.text: The bonding pair of electrons are equally shared between the atoms in?

Similar to HF, in hydrogen chloride (HCl), the bonding pair of electrons is not equally shared between the atoms. Chlorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, leading to a polar covalent bond. HCl has a partial positive charge on the hydrogen atom and a partial negative charge on the chlorine atom.

In water (H2O), the bonding pair of electrons is also not equally shared between the atoms. Oxygen is more electronegative than hydrogen, causing the oxygen atom to pull the electrons towards itself, resulting in a polar covalent bond. Water has a partial positive charge on the hydrogen atoms and a partial negative charge on the oxygen atom.

In hydrogen gas (H2), the bonding pair of electrons is equally shared between the two hydrogen atoms. Both hydrogen atoms have the same electronegativity, so they share the electrons equally, forming a nonpolar covalent bond.

questions.text: The total of all the possible kind of energies in a system is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Total energy refers to the sum of all the different types of energies present in a system. This includes both kinetic energy and potential energy.

Kinetic energy is the energy possessed by an object due to its motion. The kinetic energy of an object depends on its mass and velocity and can be calculated using the formula: KE = 0.5 * m * v^2, where m is the mass of the object and v is its velocity.

Potential energy is the energy stored in an object or system based on its position or configuration. There are different types of potential energy, such as gravitational potential energy, elastic potential energy, and chemical potential energy.

Internal energy refers to the total energy contained within a system due to the kinetic energy of its particles and the potential energy stored in the bonds between particles. It includes both the kinetic and potential energies associated with the random motion and interactions of particles within the system.

questions.explanation: Internal energy is defined as the total energy of a closed system.

questions.text: Energy is absorbed?

As a result, the temperature of the surroundings decreases, and the products of the reaction have higher energy content than the reactants.

When new chemical bonds are formed during a reaction, energy is released or given off to the surroundings. This energy is the result of potential energy being converted to kinetic energy as the atoms or molecules come together and form stable chemical bonds.

questions.explanation: Breaking bonds requires adding energy, the opposite process of forming new bonds always releases energy. Bond breaking is an endothermic process while bond formation is an exothermic process.

questions.text: Which of the following solvents favor SN2 reactions?

Carbon tetrachloride is a non-polar solvent that does not stabilize ions. However, it can be conducive to SN2 reactions as it does not interfere with the nucleophile's attack on the substrate.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Carbon tetrachloride, which is a non-polar solvent that does not stabilize ions and thus allows for a more favorable environment for SN2 reactions. In SN2 mechanisms, the nucleophile attacks the substrate in a single concerted step, and polar protic solvents like water, ammonia, and acetic acid can hinder this process by stabilizing the transition state or the leaving group, making them less favorable for such reactions.In contrast, polar aprotic solvents (like carbon tetrachloride) do not solvate the nucleophile as strongly, allowing it to remain reactive and effectively participate in the nucleophilic substitution process.

questions.text: Melting and boiling points depends on _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Melting and boiling points of substances depend on various factors, and one of the factors that play a role is the number of electrons. These forces arise due to temporary fluctuations in electron distributions, resulting in temporary dipoles. More electrons mean larger temporary dipoles and stronger intermolecular forces, leading to higher melting and boiling points.

The number of unpaired electrons also affects the properties of substances, but it is not directly related to their melting and boiling points. Unpaired electrons are electrons that are not paired with another electron in the same orbital. They play a role in chemical bonding and reactivity, but they do not significantly influence the physical properties like melting and boiling points.

The number of paired electrons is not directly related to the melting and boiling points either. Paired electrons are electrons that are paired with another electron in the same orbital, and they do not significantly affect the physical properties of substances.

The number of protons in an atom determines its atomic number and chemical identity, but it does not directly affect the melting and boiling points of substances. The properties of substances are primarily influenced by the arrangement of electrons and the interactions between molecules.

questions.text: Rate= k[NO][O3] the order of this reaction is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The given chemical reaction is: Rate = k[NO][O3] The order of a chemical reaction with respect to a specific reactant is determined by the exponent (known as the reaction order) to which the concentration of that reactant is raised in the rate equation. In the given rate equation, we have: Rate = k[NO][O3] The reaction order with respect to NO is 1 (since the concentration of NO is raised to the power of 1), and the reaction order with respect to O3 is also 1. The overall reaction order is the sum of the individual reaction orders: Overall reaction order = 1 + 1 = 2 Thus, the overall order of this reaction is 2. It is a second-order reaction with respect to the concentrations of both NO and O3.

If the rate of the reaction is not dependent on the concentration of any of the reactants, the reaction is said to be zero-order with respect to those reactants. In this case, the rate law does not contain any reactant concentrations with non-zero exponents.

If the rate of the reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of one reactant, the reaction is said to be first-order with respect to that reactant. In this case, the rate law contains a reactant concentration with an exponent of 1.

If the rate of the reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of one reactant raised to the power of 3, the reaction is said to be third-order with respect to that reactant. In this case, the rate law contains a reactant concentration with an exponent of 3.

questions.text: Glucose is converted into ethanol by the _ enzyme present in yeast.

options.explanation: Invertase, also known as sucrase, is an enzyme that catalyzes the hydrolysis of sucrose into its constituent monosaccharides, glucose, and fructose. This process is essential for the digestion and absorption of sucrose in the digestive system.

This reaction is particularly important in the nitrogen metabolism of living organisms, as it allows the conversion of urea, a nitrogen-containing compound, into ammonia, which can be used for various cellular processes.

Glycolysis is the first step of cellular respiration, in which one molecule of glucose (a 6-carbon sugar) is broken down into two molecules of pyruvate (3-carbon each). It takes place in the cytoplasm of the cell and does not require oxygen (anaerobic process).

Zymase is an enzyme that catalyzes the conversion of glucose into ethanol. Glucose undergoes fermentation to be converted into ethanol, a process that naturally occurs in yeasts. questions.text: Among halogens, iodine is solid at room temperature while Br2 is liquid and Cl2 and F2 are gases at room temperature, why?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Dipole forces, also known as dipole-dipole forces, occur between polar molecules. These forces are relatively weak compared to other intermolecular forces. Halogens (like Cl2, Br2, and I2) are nonpolar molecules, so they do not have permanent dipoles. Therefore, the differences in dipole forces do not explain the varying states of the halogens.

Polarity refers to the distribution of charge in a molecule. Polar molecules have an uneven distribution of charge, resulting in a positive and a negative end. Nonpolar molecules, on the other hand, have an even distribution of charge. In the case of halogens, Cl2 and Br2 are nonpolar molecules, while I2 is a slightly polar molecule due to its larger size and greater electron-electron repulsion. However, the polarity of I2 is not significant enough to explain its solid state at room temperature compared to the other halogens.

London dispersion forces (also called van der Waals forces) are the weakest intermolecular forces. They arise due to temporary fluctuations in electron distribution that create temporary dipoles in nonpolar molecules. As the size and number of electrons in a molecule increase, the London forces become stronger. In the halogens, the molecular weight increases from F2 to Cl2 to Br2 to I2, and consequently, the strength of London forces also increases. This increase in London forces is the primary reason for the change in states of halogens at room temperature.

Debye forces are another type of intermolecular force that occurs in polar molecules with permanent dipoles. These forces are not significant contributors to the states of halogens, as Cl2 and Br2 are nonpolar, and F2 is only slightly polar.

questions.explanation: As you move down the Group, the attraction forces between the molecules of these elements get stronger. Each substance's molecules are attracted to one another by dispersion (London) intermolecular forces. The balance between the kinetic energy of the molecules and their intermolecular attractions determines whether a material is a solid, liquid, or gas. The electrons in fluorine are firmly bound to the nuclei. The London dispersion forces are generally modest because electrons have limited opportunity to stray to one side of the molecule. The electrons are more away from the nuclei as we progress from fluorine to iodine, allowing the electron clouds to deform more easily. The dispersion forces in London grow increasingly powerful. The molecules will all be solids at a low enough temperature. They'll all be gases at a high enough temperature. Now Because the fluorine molecule has a low molecular weight and weak intermolecular interactions, it exists as a gas at ambient temperature. Bromine, on the other hand, has a slightly higher molecular weight than fluorine and has stronger intermolecular interactions, thus it persists as a liquid at ambient temperature. Because iodine has a large molecular weight and strong Van Der Waals forces, it exists as a solid at normal temperature.

questions.text: Full wave bridge rectifier uses:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A full wave rectifier cannot be implemented using only one diode. A diode is a semiconductor device that allows current to flow in one direction and blocks it in the opposite direction. For a full wave rectification, which means converting an AC (alternating current) signal into a pulsating DC (direct current) signal, we need to utilize both the positive and negative cycles of the AC signal. A single diode can only rectify either the positive or the negative half-cycle, not both.

A full wave rectifier typically uses two diodes arranged in a bridge configuration called a "bridge rectifier" or "full-wave bridge rectifier." This configuration allows both the positive and negative halves of the AC signal to be rectified, resulting in a pulsating DC output.

A configuration with three diodes cannot produce full wave rectification. While it is possible to design various rectifier circuits with three diodes (such as center-tapped rectifiers), these configurations typically provide half-wave rectification rather than full wave.

questions.text: A star moving away from earth shows?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Green shift is not a term used in astronomy. The common terms used are "red shift" and "blue shift," which refer to the apparent change in the wavelength of light from a celestial object due to its motion relative to the observer.

Red shift occurs when a celestial object is moving away from the observer (in this case, Earth). As the object moves away, the wavelength of its light appears to stretch out or increase, shifting towards the longer-wavelength (redder) end of the spectrum. Red shift is a key observation in cosmology and is associated with the expansion of the universe.

Blue shift occurs when a celestial object is moving towards the observer. As the object moves closer, the wavelength of its light appears to compress or decrease, shifting towards the shorter-wavelength (bluer) end of the spectrum. Blue shift is also observed in cosmology, especially in cases of objects moving towards us, such as certain stars or galaxies.

options.text: None of these options is correct

The correct answer to the question is one of the two shifts (red shift or blue shift) depending on the direction of motion of the star.

questions.explanation: When a star emits light, the color of its light as observed on earth depends on its motion relative to earth. If a star is moving towards the earth then its light is shifted to higher frequencies on the color spectrum (towards the gamma-ray end of the spectrum). This shift towards higher frequency is called a "blue shift". The faster a star moves towards the earth, the more its light is shifted to higher frequencies. In contrast, if a star is moving away from the earth, its light is shifted to lower frequencies on the color spectrum (towards the orange/red/infrared/microwave/radio end of the spectrum). A lower frequency shift is called a "redshift". The faster a star moves away from the earth, the more its light is shifted to lower-frequency colors. This effect is known as the "Doppler shift".

questions.text: Work done by the centripetal force on a body moving in circle is zero because?

options.text: The body moves parallel to F
options.explanation: If the body moves parallel to the centripetal force (F), then the work done by the centripetal force will be zero. However, this is not the reason why the work done by the centripetal force on a body moving in a circle is zero. In a circular motion, the centripetal force is always directed toward the center of the circle, and the body moves at right angles to this force.

options.text: The body moves opposite to F
options.explanation: If the body moves opposite to the centripetal force, then the work done by the centripetal force would be negative. However, this is not the case for circular motion. The body always moves in the direction of the centripetal force, toward the center of the circle.

options.text: The body moves at right angles to F
options.explanation: This option correctly explains why the work done by the centripetal force is zero. In circular motion, the centripetal force is always directed toward the center of the circle, while the body moves at right angles to this force. Since the force and the displacement are perpendicular to each other, the work done is zero.

options.text: The centripetal and centrifugal forces balance each other
options.explanation: The concept of "centrifugal force" is often used in non-inertial reference frames but is not an actual force. The apparent "outward" force experienced in a rotating reference frame is due to the inertia of the object trying to move in a straight line tangent to the circle. The centripetal force is the real force that keeps the body moving in a circle. There is no balance between centripetal and centrifugal forces; rather, the centripetal force is responsible for keeping the body in circular motion.

questions.text: Spectra corresponding to the sodium vapor lamp is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Band spectra are characterised by a series of broad and continuous bands of colours or wavelengths. These spectra are typically observed in molecules and complex substances, where the energy levels are closely spaced and not well-defined. Sodium vapour lamp spectra, however, do not exhibit band spectra.

Line spectra consist of discrete and distinct lines of colours or wavelengths. Each line corresponds to a specific energy transition in an atom or molecule. Sodium vapour lamps produce line spectra, where the emitted light consists of a few sharp, bright lines. These lines are a result of electronic transitions in sodium atoms when they absorb and emit photons of specific energies.

Emission spectra are produced when a substance emits light after being excited, typically by heat or electricity. Sodium vapour lamps are a common example of a light source that emits light through an emission spectrum. When an electric current passes through the sodium vapour, sodium atoms get excited, and as they return to their lower energy states, they emit light in the form of specific wavelengths or lines.

Absorption spectra, on the other hand, are produced when a substance absorbs specific wavelengths of light and appears dark or absorbs light at certain points on a continuous spectrum. This occurs when light passes through a medium, and certain wavelengths are absorbed by the medium. Sodium vapour lamps do not exhibit absorption spectra.

options.explanation: The photoelectric effect is the phenomenon where electrons are emitted from a material (usually a metal) when it is exposed to light or electromagnetic radiation of a sufficiently high frequency. The energy of the incident photons is absorbed by the material, causing electrons to be ejected from its surface. The photoelectric effect is the principle on which photocells are based. In a photocell, incident light causes the emission of electrons, generating an electric current.

questions.text: An electron cannot reside inside a nucleus is explained by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The Compton effect is a phenomenon where X-ray or gamma-ray photons are scattered by electrons. It involves the interaction between photons and electrons, leading to a change in the wavelength of the incident photons. This effect is essential for understanding the wave-particle duality of light and its interaction with matter, but it does not directly explain why electrons cannot reside inside a nucleus.

The photoelectric effect is a phenomenon in which electrons are emitted from a material when it absorbs photons of sufficient energy. This effect provided significant evidence for the particle nature of light and was explained by Albert Einstein's theory of photons. While the photoelectric effect is fundamental in understanding the behavior of electrons, it also does not directly explain why electrons cannot reside inside a nucleus.

The Zeeman effect is the splitting of spectral lines in the presence of a magnetic field. It is observed when the energy levels of atoms or molecules are affected by the magnetic field, leading to the splitting of spectral lines. While this effect is critical in understanding the behavior of atomic and molecular spectra, it does not address why electrons cannot reside inside a nucleus.

The uncertainty principle, formulated by Werner Heisenberg, is a fundamental principle of quantum mechanics. It states that there is a limit to how precisely certain pairs of physical properties, such as position and momentum, can be simultaneously known. In the case of the electron's position and momentum, the more accurately we know one of these properties, the less accurately we can know the other. This principle implies that it is impossible to precisely locate an electron inside a nucleus, which leads to the understanding that electrons cannot reside inside the nucleus.

questions.explanation: The Uncertainty Principle (also called the Heisenberg uncertainty principle or indeterminacy principle) was articulated by the German physicist Werner Heisenberg in 1927, and it stated that the more precisely the position of a particle is known the more uncertain the momentum is and vice versa. The Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle explains why electrons do not fall into the nucleus of an atom. The principle especially states that the product of the uncertainty of position and the uncertainty of momentum is greater than or equal to Planck's reduced constant divided by two.The diameter of the atomic nucleus is of the order of 10-15mTherefore, for an electron to exist inside the nucleus the maximum uncertainty in the position of an electron is 10-15m

questions.text: 1 light year distance is the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Distance travelled by earth in one year
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Earth travels approximately one astronomical unit (AU) in its orbit around the Sun in one year, which is about 149.6 million kilometers. A light-year is much greater than the distance Earth travels in a year.

options.text: Distance travelled by a star in one year
options.explanation: While stars do move through space, their motion over one year is typically much less than the vast distances represented by a light-year. Stars can have relative motions due to their orbits within galaxies or through interactions with other celestial bodies, but this motion is usually negligible compared to the scale of a light-year.

options.text: Distance travelled by light in one year
options.explanation: This statement is correct. A light-year is defined as the distance that light travels in one year in a vacuum. Since light travels at a constant speed of approximately 299,792 kilometers per second (in vacuum), the distance it travels in one year is about 9.461 trillion kilometres (or about 5.879 trillion miles).

options.text: Distance travelled by light in one galactic year
options.explanation: A galactic year, also known as a cosmic year, is the time it takes for the Sun to complete one orbit around the center of the Milky Way galaxy. This period is estimated to be about 225-250 million Earth years.Light-year is not defined with respect to a galactic year. It is solely based on the distance light travels in one year.

questions.explanation: A light-year is defined as the distance that light travels in one year in a vacuum.
